mnemonics.title: PIP Steps
mnemonics.content: PIP = PyPI (Python Package Index) Installation Process: (1) Download, (2) Install, (3) Verify with --version

mnemonics.title: VENV Command
mnemonics.content: Create Virtual Environment: python -m venv [name] → activate → pip install → work → deactivate

mnemonics.title: PATH Purpose
mnemonics.content: PATH = Puts All Tool Hosts Everywhere - allows Python commands from any directory location

acronyms.usage: Package manager for downloading Python libraries like RPi.GPIO, requests for IoT projects
Isolated workspace for each IoT project to manage dependencies separately
System variable telling OS where to find executable programs like python.exe
Online repository of Python packages - pip downloads packages from pypi.org
Pins on Raspberry Pi for connecting sensors/actuators in IoT applications

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a house (project folder) with its own utility room (venv) containing only the tools (packages) needed for that specific IoT project, separate from the main house (system Python)
Think of PATH as a street map - without it, Python is like a house with no address; the OS can't find it unless you give the exact street coordinates (full path)
pip is like an app store - you search for packages (apps), click install, and they download with all dependencies automatically

analogies.analogy: Installing Python is like building a kitchen before cooking - you need the basic setup (interpreter) before you can prepare any IoT meals (projects)
Virtual environments are like separate laboratories - Experiment A (Project 1) using Chemistry v2.0 won't contaminate Experiment B (Project 2) using Chemistry v3.0
pip is like a personal shopping assistant - you tell it what IoT package you want, and it finds it from the Python Package Index warehouse and delivers it to your project
